eventMetadata.context_description: Andrei Vasilevskiy's league-leading .925 save percentage and 2.30 goals-against average anchor his frontrunner status at 62.6% implied probability, bolstered by his post-injury surge since late February where he's posted a .938 SV% over 15 starts, fueling Tampa Bay's wild-card push. Ilya Sorokin's .923 SV% and 26 wins sustain his 29.5% share, reflecting New York Islanders' defensive structure amid their Metropolitan Division battle. Jeremy Swayman's 6.7% reflects Boston Bruins' tandem success, with his .917 SV% and 23 victories in 42 games amid Linus Ullmark's shared workload. Trader consensus weighs quality starts, shutouts, and playoff implications, though late surges or injuries could shift dynamics as the regular season nears its close.

question: Will Rachida Dati win the Paris mayor election?
description:
The 2026 election for the Mayor of Paris is scheduled to take place in March.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the 2026 Paris mayoral election to become the next elected Mayor of Paris.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder mayors appointed before the election will not be considered.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

events.title: Paris Mayoral Election
events.description:
The 2026 election for the Mayor of Paris is scheduled to take place in March.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the 2026 Paris mayoral election to become the next elected Mayor of Paris.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder mayors appointed before the election will not be considered.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly backs Emmanuel Grégoire at 100% implied probability to win the 2026 Paris mayoral election, driven by the Socialist Party's entrenched control of the city council, where the mayor is elected by councilors following municipal balloting. As Paris's first deputy mayor and PS standard-bearer after Anne Hidalgo's decision not to seek a third term, Grégoire benefits from incumbency advantages, favorable local polling leads over rivals like Rachida Dati of Les Républicains and Sophia Chikirou of La France Insoumise, and limited left-wing fragmentation. The 33% on Éric Grégoire reflects minor speculative interest in an outsider challenge. Realistic disruptions could include a unified center-right coalition, a major scandal, or a national political shift boosting opposition turnout in the March 2026 vote.

question: Will the Boston Bruins make the NHL Playoffs?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed team makes the 2025–26 NHL Playoffs. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

A team will be considered to have made the Playoffs if it qualifies for the postseason bracket under the official rules of the NHL for the 2025–26 season. Qualification through any officially recognized NHL format (including Wild Card berths) will count.

If it becomes impossible for the listed team to qualify for the Playoffs based on the rules of the NHL (e.g. the team is mathematically eliminated), this market will resolve immediately to “No.”

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NHL, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the NHL regular season entering its final week, trader consensus locks in playoff berths for eight teams—Boston, Florida, Toronto, NY Rangers, Carolina, Tampa Bay, Dallas, and Vancouver—while intense wild-card races dominate sentiment in both conferences. In the East, the Islanders hold a slim edge over Detroit and Philadelphia thanks to superior regulation wins (ROW), bolstered by recent Isles surges and Philly's injury woes with Carter Hart sidelined. Western wild cards pit Minnesota against St. Louis and Vegas, where the Wild's rest advantage and Vegas's road slump shift probabilities. Momentum favors hot streaks like Detroit's five-game win run, but brutal closing schedules, including back-to-backs and rivalry clashes, could flip bubble outcomes unpredictably.

question: Billionaire one-time wealth tax on California ballot?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a ballot initiative is certified to appear on the official statewide California ballot for the November 3, 2026 election, that proposes a one-time tax targeting individuals, households, or family units with wealth, assets, or net worth of at least $1 billion (USD or equivalent), by June 25, 2026, 11:59 PM ET (the official cutoff date for new initiatives to be approved). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Certification means the initiative is officially approved by the California Secretary of State for a statewide ballot.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the government of California. If unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

events.title: Billionaire one-time wealth tax on California ballot?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a ballot initiative is certified to appear on the official statewide California ballot for the November 3, 2026 election, that proposes a one-time tax targeting individuals, households, or family units with wealth, assets, or net worth of at least $1 billion (USD or equivalent), by June 25, 2026, 11:59 PM ET (the official cutoff date for new initiatives to be approved). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Certification means the initiative is officially approved by the California Secretary of State for a statewide ballot.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the government of California. If unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a 61% implied probability for a billionaire one-time wealth tax measure qualifying for the California ballot, driven primarily by proponents' submission of over 700,000 signatures last week to the Secretary of State's office, surpassing the required 546,651 valid signatures for 2026 ballot placement. Verification remains pending, with historical data showing about 70-80% validation rates for similar initiatives, bolstering optimism. Recent endorsements from labor unions and progressive groups, alongside polls indicating 55-60% voter support amid budget deficit pressures, have fueled upward momentum, though opponents cite legal hurdles over the tax's structure on unrealized gains. No official qualification yet, but upcoming signature audits could shift odds.

question: Billionaire one-time wealth tax passes in California election 2026?
description:
A one-time wealth tax on billionaires has been proposed to potentially appear on California's ballot for the November 3, 2026 general election. You can read more about that here: https://6abc.com/post/california-union-proposes-taxing-billionaires-offset-medicaid-cuts-low-income-people/18066430/

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any proposition containing a one-time tax targeting individuals, households, or family units with wealth, assets, or net worth of at least $1 billion (USD or equivalent) passes in the named election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If no qualifying ballot initiative is certified to appear on the official statewide California ballot as a proposition to be voted on in the stated election by June 25, 2026, 11:59 PM ET (the official cutoff date for new initiatives to be approved), or if all qualifying propositions/initiatives are removed from the ballot or amended before the election such that the main threshold drops below $1 billion, this market will resolve "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of the State of California, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Billionaire one-time wealth tax passes in California election 2026?
events.description:
A one-time wealth tax on billionaires has been proposed to potentially appear on California's ballot for the November 3, 2026 general election. You can read more about that here: https://6abc.com/post/california-union-proposes-taxing-billionaires-offset-medicaid-cuts-low-income-people/18066430/

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any proposition containing a one-time tax targeting individuals, households, or family units with wealth, assets, or net worth of at least $1 billion (USD or equivalent) passes in the named election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If no qualifying ballot initiative is certified to appear on the official statewide California ballot as a proposition to be voted on in the stated election by June 25, 2026, 11:59 PM ET (the official cutoff date for new initiatives to be approved), or if all qualifying propositions/initiatives are removed from the ballot or amended before the election such that the main threshold drops below $1 billion, this market will resolve "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of the State of California, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus leans against passage of a one-time wealth tax on California billionaires in the 2026 election, with "No" shares at 65.5%, reflecting skepticism over ballot qualification and voter support amid economic headwinds. The proposed measure, backed by progressive groups seeking funds for housing and education, has gathered initial signatures but faces hurdles in reaching the 546,651 needed by July 2026, per state rules. Recent polls show tepid support below 50%, hampered by business opposition, resident exodus concerns, and voter wariness after narrow 2024 tax-related defeats elsewhere. Historical ballot rejections of wealth taxes reinforce doubts, though Democratic supermajorities could aid if it qualifies.

question: US x Russia military clash by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of the United States and Russia between May 28 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between U.S. and Russian military forces. Non-violent actions, such as airspace violations, firing of warning shots (such as the June, 2021 Black Sea Confrontations between Russian forces and HMS Defender), or cyberattacks will not qualify. 

Intentional physical collisions, including aerial interceptions and naval ramming without the direct use of weaponry, such as the 2023 Black Sea incident—where a Russian Su-27 damaged a U.S. MQ-9 Reaper drone's propeller, leading to its crash— will not qualify regardless of damage. 

Military contractors will qualify only if confirmed to be operating under the direct command or coordination of the respective state’s armed forces (e.g. the Battle of Khasham would not qualify).

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: US x Russia military clash by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of the United States and Russia between May 28 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between U.S. and Russian military forces. Non-violent actions, such as airspace violations, firing of warning shots (such as the June, 2021 Black Sea Confrontations between Russian forces and HMS Defender), or cyberattacks will not qualify. 

Intentional physical collisions, including aerial interceptions and naval ramming without the direct use of weaponry, such as the 2023 Black Sea incident—where a Russian Su-27 damaged a U.S. MQ-9 Reaper drone's propeller, leading to its crash— will not qualify regardless of damage. 

Military contractors will qualify only if confirmed to be operating under the direct command or coordination of the respective state’s armed forces (e.g. the Battle of Khasham would not qualify).

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probability for a direct US-Russia military clash, driven by Washington's policy of arming Ukraine without committing troops, maintaining a proxy conflict buffer. Recent US approval for ATACMS missile strikes on Russian territory in October 2024 elicited Kremlin warnings of escalation but no retaliatory strikes on NATO assets, underscoring mutual deterrence. North Korean troop reinforcements in Russia's Kursk region add indirect pressure, yet both sides prioritize de-escalation rhetoric. Upcoming Trump administration transition in January 2025 and potential Ukraine peace talks could further dampen risks, though Ukrainian frontline advances remain a wildcard amid volatile rhetoric.

question: Claudia Sheinbaum out as President of Mexico by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Claudia Sheinbaum ceases to be President of Mexico for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Claudia Sheinbaum's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Claudia Sheinbaum and the government of Mexico; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Claudia Sheinbaum out as President of Mexico by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Claudia Sheinbaum Pardo announces she is resigning as President of Mexico or otherwise ceases to be President of Mexico for any length of time by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement of Claudia Sheinbaum’s resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from Claudia Sheinbaum or the Government of Mexico, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Claudia Sheinbaum's solid hold on Mexico's presidency, backed by Morena's congressional supermajority, drives low trader odds of her early exit, as impeachment requires unlikely two-thirds support for serious offenses. Her October 2024 inauguration followed a landslide win, with approval ratings near 75% per recent Mitofsky polls amid policy continuity from predecessor López Obrador. Judicial reform debates, including elected judges and Supreme Court pushback, have fueled protests and U.S. investor concerns, but no removal mechanisms have activated. Persistent cartel violence and fiscal pressures test stability, yet no scandals threaten tenure. Traders watch December budget votes and 2025 energy reforms for coalition strains, aligning with historical rarity of mid-term presidential ousters.

question: Will Gavin Newsom announce a Presidential run by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Gavin Newsom officially announces that he is running for U.S. President in the 2028 presidential election, by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

For the purpose of resolving this market, an announcement by Gavin Newsom will be sufficient to trigger a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether he actually filed a nomination to run, or whether he actually files a nomination to run in the future.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements by Gavin Newsom (e.g., via speech, social media, etc.) or his official or legal representatives; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements by Gavin Newsom (e.g., via speech, social media, etc.) or his official or legal representatives; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom's firm endorsement of Kamala Harris and focus on California's budget deficit and wildfire response have kept presidential speculation dormant, driving low trader consensus for an announcement by year-end. Post-November 5 election outcomes could reshape 2028 primary fields if Harris falters, but Newsom's term-limited governorship ends in 2026 without signals of a White House bid. Traders weigh his state duties against historical patterns where governors test waters subtly before formal launches.

question: Will Ari Weinstein leave OpenAI by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Ari Weinstein ceases any employment or formal contractual involvement with OpenAI, for any length of time by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

question: Will Liam rank #1 among boy names on the SSA’s official list for 2025?
description:
This market will resolve according to the male name ranked #1 in the U.S. Social Security Administration’s (SSA) official list of popular baby names for 2025, published on the SSA website: https://www.ssa.gov/oact/babynames/.

The authoritative source is the SSA’s “Popular Names by Birth Year” tool. To view the relevant "Male name" list, set the Birth Year to 2025.

This market may resolve as soon as the SSA releases its 2025 name data for the specified year. If none of the listed options is ranked #1 or if the SSA does not release the 2025 rankings by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

eventMetadata.context_description: Liam's commanding 87.6% implied probability as the most popular boy name for 2025 stems from its unchallenged reign atop SSA rankings since 2017, with over 20,000 annual registrations in recent years, outpacing rivals amid stable millennial parenting preferences. Noah trails at 9.8% buoyed by biblical appeal and consistent top-three finishes, while risers like Theodore (0.5%) gain from prestige trends in media like Ted Lasso. eventMetadata.context_description: Olivia's commanding 84.5% implied probability on Polymarket stems from its unchallenged reign as America's top girl name for five straight years per the Social Security Administration's (SSA) 2023 data, released in May 2024, where it led Emma by nearly 2,000 registrations. This numerical dominance—14,581 Olivias versus Emma's 12,558—fuels trader consensus that trends will hold into 2025 births, absent major cultural disruptions. Emma clings to 6.6% as a perennial runner-up, while Amelia's 4.2% reflects its steady climb into the top five. Slower risers like Mia and Charlotte trail due to narrower gaps, with SSA's May 2026 release of 2025 data as the key resolution trigger; pop culture echoes from Olivia Rodrigo sustain the frontrunner's edge without sparking rivals.

This market will resolve according to the female name ranked #1 in the U.S. Social Security Administration’s (SSA) official list of popular baby names for 2025, published on the SSA website: https://www.ssa.gov/oact/babynames/.

The authoritative source is the SSA’s “Popular Names by Birth Year” tool. To view the relevant "Female name" list, set the Birth Year to 2025.

This market may resolve as soon as the SSA releases its 2025 name data for the specified year. If none of the listed options is ranked #1 or if the SSA does not release the 2025 rankings by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

events.title: Most popular girl name 2025

eventMetadata.context_description: Olivia's commanding 84.5% implied probability on Polymarket stems from its unchallenged reign as America's top girl name for five straight years per the Social Security Administration's (SSA) 2023 data, released in May 2024, where it led Emma by nearly 2,000 registrations. This numerical dominance—14,581 Olivias versus Emma's 12,558—fuels trader consensus that trends will hold into 2025 births, absent major cultural disruptions. Emma clings to 6.6% as a perennial runner-up, while Amelia's 4.2% reflects its steady climb into the top five. Slower risers like Mia and Charlotte trail due to narrower gaps, with SSA's May 2026 release of 2025 data as the key resolution trigger; pop culture echoes from Olivia Rodrigo sustain the frontrunner's edge without sparking rivals.

question: OpenAI announces it has achieved AGI before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if OpenAI or an official representative of the company announces that it has created an artificial general intelligence (AGI) by December 31, 2026 ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from OpenAI and/or its official representative, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: OpenAI announces it has achieved AGI before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if OpenAI or an official representative of the company announces that it has created an artificial general intelligence (AGI) by December 31, 2026 ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from OpenAI and/or its official representative, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders overwhelmingly back "No" at 85% implied probability, driven by OpenAI's lack of any official AGI announcement despite hype around models like o1-preview, which excels in reasoning benchmarks but falls short of AGI's demanding threshold—systems outperforming humans across most economically valuable work. Recent developments, including Sam Altman's interviews pegging AGI timelines at 2025-2030 without claiming achievement, reinforce skepticism, as o1's capabilities remain narrow compared to broad superintelligence. Key catalysts include anticipated GPT-5/Orion launches in 2025 and expert surveys forecasting median AGI arrival in the 2030s, amid regulatory scrutiny delaying bold claims in a competitive landscape with Anthropic and Google.

question: Will Jia Yueting enter mainland China by June 30?
description:
If Jia Yueting enters mainland China by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, "entering mainland China" is defined as Jia Yueting physically setting foot within the territory of the People’s Republic of China, excluding Hong Kong, Macau, and Taiwan.

Travel through airspace or territorial waters during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Will Jia Yueting enter mainland China by...?
events.description:
If Jia Yueting enters mainland China by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, "entering mainland China" is defined as Jia Yueting physically setting foot within the territory of the People’s Republic of China, excluding Hong Kong, Macau, and Taiwan.

Travel through airspace or territorial waters during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Jia Yueting's potential entry into mainland China hinges on his longstanding fugitive status, with Chinese authorities listing him since 2017 for unpaid debts exceeding 12 billion yuan from LeEco's collapse. Recent social media statements from Yueting express willingness to return and negotiate repayments, bolstered by Faraday Future's ongoing U.S. operations and stock volatility, yet no official permissions or travel confirmations have emerged. Skepticism persists amid strict enforcement on financial fugitives, though EV sector incentives could encourage leniency. Key watchpoints include any announcements from Chinese courts or Yueting's X updates, as diplomatic or economic shifts might sway probabilities before the resolution date.

question: Will OpenAI IPO by June 30 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if OpenAI completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) by the listed date ET, as confirmed by official company announcements and credible news sources. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The IPO refers to the first sale of stock by the listed company to the public on any recognized stock exchange.

If OpenAI is acquired by another company that is already public, this market will immediately resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: OpenAI IPO by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if OpenAI completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) by the listed date ET, as confirmed by official company announcements and credible news sources. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The IPO refers to the first sale of stock by the listed company to the public on any recognized stock exchange.

If OpenAI is acquired by another company that is already public, this market will immediately resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for an OpenAI IPO remains subdued, with market-implied probabilities hovering below 15% for year-end 2024, primarily driven by CEO Sam Altman's repeated statements ruling out near-term public listing amid the company's shift to a for-profit structure announced in September. This restructuring aims to attract more institutional capital from backers like Microsoft without diluting control, but it underscores OpenAI's preference for private funding amid sky-high valuations exceeding $150 billion. Competitive pressures from Anthropic and xAI intensify innovation focus over exit strategies, while regulatory scrutiny from the FTC and EU on AI dominance adds hurdles. Key watchpoints include Q4 funding rounds or board updates that could signal IPO readiness, though historical delays in Big Tech listings suggest caution.

question: OpenAI $1t+ IPO before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if OpenAI completes an initial public offering (IPO) valued at $1 trillion USD or higher at the time of the IPO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

An “initial public offering (IPO)” refers to the first sale of OpenAI’s equity securities to the public through a regulated stock exchange.

OpenAI will be considered to have achieved a $1 trillion valuation if the market capitalization implied by the IPO offering price multiplied by the total number of outstanding shares equals or exceeds $1 trillion USD.

Announcements, filings, or planned IPOs that do not result in public trading by that time will not qualify. Private funding rounds, secondary share sales, or employee-share transactions will not be considered. A direct listing or merger via SPAC will qualify only if it results in OpenAI’s common shares becoming publicly traded for the first time on a major exchange.

If OpenAI’s IPO is priced before the resolution deadline but public trading has not yet commenced, the market may remain open for up to 30 calendar days to determine whether the IPO is completed.

If OpenAI is acquired, dissolved, or merged into another entity before an IPO occurs, this market will resolve to “No.” In the event of a restructuring, the market will resolve based on the entity legally recognized as OpenAI’s successor will 

The resolution source will be a consensus for credible reporting.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 80% implied probability for OpenAI reaching a $1 trillion-plus market cap via IPO before 2027, driven primarily by the firm's current $157 billion private valuation—set in its October 2024 funding round—and the absence of any official IPO filing or timeline amid a compressed two-year window. Recent developments, including OpenAI's September board-approved shift to a public benefit corporation for-profit structure, signal preparation for eventual public markets but underscore execution risks like soaring compute costs, regulatory scrutiny from FTC probes and AI safety mandates, and intensifying competition from Google DeepMind and xAI. Without demonstrated trillion-dollar revenue trajectories or resolved governance issues, traders see historical private-to-public valuation multiples (often compressing) as insufficient to bridge the gap before 2027.

question: Will Argentina dollarize by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Argentina begins to peg its currency to the United States dollar (USD), or adopts the United States dollar as official legal tender, June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement that dollarization will begin will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes" - for this market to resolve to "Yes", dollarization must have actually begun.

Note: a peg does not need to be 1:1 to USD.

This market's resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting indicating either of the listed scenarios have begun.

events.title: Will Argentina dollarize by June 30, 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Argentina begins to peg its currency to the United States dollar (USD), or adopts the United States dollar as official legal tender, June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement that dollarization will begin will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes" - for this market to resolve to "Yes", dollarization must have actually begun.

Note: a peg does not need to be 1:1 to USD.

This market's resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting indicating either of the listed scenarios have begun.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Javier Milei's administration has prioritized fiscal discipline and peso stabilization over rapid dollarization, achieving a primary fiscal surplus for the first time in over a decade and reducing monthly inflation to single digits by mid-2025, diminishing the urgency for adopting the U.S. dollar. Traders' 97.7% consensus on "No" reflects insurmountable hurdles: Congress, where Milei's La Libertad Avanza holds a minority, must approve any currency regime change, while net dollar reserves remain deeply negative—far short of the $40 billion needed for dollarization. Recent IMF talks emphasize gradual reforms without endorsing dollarization. Realistic shifts could involve midterm election gains yielding legislative majorities or export booms building reserves, though these appear improbable by June 2026.

question: Will Purdue win the 2026 NCAA Tournament?
description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2026 NCAA Men’s Basketball Tournament.

If it is impossible for a team to win the 2026 NCAA Men’s Basketball Tournament according to the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated) , the corresponding market will immediately resolve to “No”.

If for any reason no winner is declared by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NCAA.

events.title: 2026 NCAA Tournament Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2026 NCAA Men’s Basketball Tournament.

If it is impossible for a team to win the 2026 NCAA Men’s Basketball Tournament according to the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated) , the corresponding market will immediately resolve to “No”.

If for any reason no winner is declared by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NCAA.

eventMetadata.context_description: Michigan's blockbuster No. 1 recruiting class under new coach Dusty May has edged it to the top of 2026 NCAA Tournament winner odds at 20.5%, but Arizona (19.4%) and Duke (16.5%) keep the market bunched tight through elite talent pipelines of their own. Arizona reloads via transfer portal savvy and top-5 freshmen, while Duke banks on Cooper Flagg's sophomore dominance alongside incoming McDonald's All-Americans. Florida and Houston trail with battle-tested cores and defensive identities, but pervasive uncertainty—two-way transfers, NBA draft decisions, early-season chemistry—fuels trader consensus on parity, mirroring college hoops' history of mid-major upsets and one-and-done volatility.

eventMetadata.context_description: UConn's commanding 66.5% implied probability in the 2026 Women's NCAA Tournament winner market reflects their top-ranked 2025 recruiting class, headlined by five-star phenom Sarah Strong and a deep haul of elite guards and wings, bolstering a roster potentially featuring a fifth-year Paige Bueckers after her dominant return this season. Recent commitments in the winter signing period have widened UConn's talent edge, with trader consensus pricing in their historical March dominance amid roster continuity for all top contenders.

If it becomes impossible for a team to win the 2026 NCAA Women’s Basketball Tournament according to the rules of the competition (i.e., the team is eliminated), the corresponding market will immediately resolve to “No.”

If for any reason no winner is declared by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NCAA.

UCLA's 14% share gains from their No. 2 recruiting haul and West Coast pipeline under Cori Close, while South Carolina (10.1%) and Texas (8.2%) hold firm on Dawn Staley's championship pedigree and Vic Schaefer's transfer portal savvy, respectively, despite graduations and WNBA departures. question: Will Trump meet with Putin by March 31, 2026?
description:
On August 15, 2025, U.S. President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin met in person at Joint Base Elmendorf-Richardson near Anchorage, Alaska, for a summit focused on potential peace terms in Ukraine. 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Vladimir Putin meets with Donald Trump on a separate occasion from the August 15, 2025 meeting by the listed date (ET).  Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Encounters that are part of, or a continuation of, the August 15 meeting will not qualify, even if they occur on a different day (e.g. August 16).

A meeting is defined as any encounter where both Putin and Trump are present and interact with each other in person.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Watch for Ukraine frontline shifts or early administration signals that could accelerate Russia-U.S. summitry.

question: Will the U.S. invade Venezuela by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Venezuela between November 3, 2025 and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by Venezuela or the United States as of September 6, 2025, 12:00 PM ET, will be considered the sovereign territory of that country.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

question: Will Mamdani make NYC buses free by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes”  if both the following occur: 

1. Zohran Mamdani wins the 2025 NYC Mayoral election. 

2. The New York City Metropolitan Transportation Authority implements a policy under which the base local fare for regularly scheduled MTA bus routes operating within New York City (New York City Transit and MTA Bus Company) is $0 by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

The policy will be considered to have been implemented if it is actively in effect by the resolution date. The enactment of a policy without its implementation will not alone qualify. Policies which are blocked by legal or other means before they go into effect will similarly not qualify.  

Limited pilots (e.g., a subset of routes or specific days), temporary “fare holidays,” or targeted free-fare programs (e.g., only youth, seniors, or low-income riders) do not qualify.

Policies which include limited exceptions such as express-bus fares, fares on specific lines (such as Airport lines) or other exclusions will still qualify as long as a general policy of $0 fare  for regularly scheduled MTA bus routes is in effect. 

If Mamdani is confirmed to have lost the 2025 NYC Mayoral election by a consensus of credible reporting this market will immediately resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 98.7% implied probability that New York State Assemblymember Zohran Mamdani will not secure fare-free NYC buses by March 31, driven by stalled legislative progress on his repeated proposals to redirect state funds to offset MTA fare revenue losses of around $630 million annually. The 2025 state budget, finalized last spring without this provision, shows no signs of supplemental allocation amid opposition from Governor Hochul, Mayor Adams, and MTA leadership citing fiscal constraints. question: Will the Iranian regime fall before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects the Iranian regime's demonstrated resilience, with the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) maintaining tight control amid economic sanctions and sporadic protests. Recent developments, including successful suppression of dissent following the 2022 Mahsa Amini unrest—through arrests, executions, and internet blackouts—have quelled widespread uprisings, justifying the 62.5% "No" odds for regime collapse before 2027. External tensions, like Israel's April 2024 strikes on Iranian assets and Tehran's restrained missile responses, have rallied nationalist support without triggering internal fractures. Supreme Leader Khamenei's opaque health rumors persist but show no evidence of succession crisis, while proxy militias bolster regime projection. Upcoming nuclear talks and U.S. election dynamics add uncertainty but favor continuity.

question: Mamdani opens city-owned grocery store  by June 30?
description:
Zohran Mamdani has proposed creating city owned grocery stores to combat rising prices (see: https://www.nytimes.com/2024/12/12/nyregion/grocery-stores-city-owned.html).

This market will resolve to “Yes” if both the following occur:

1. Zohran Mamdani wins the 2025 NYC Mayoral election.

2. The City of New York implements a policy under which at least one grocery store that is city-owned and operated is actively open to the public by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The policy will be considered to have been implemented if at least one such store is open for regular grocery retail sales to the public by the resolution date. The enactment or announcement of a policy without an operational, open store will not alone qualify. Policies that are blocked, cancelled, or not yet in effect by the resolution date will similarly not qualify.

Limited pilots, studies, planning initiatives, or temporary pop-up markets which don’t regularly operate as regular grocery stores do not qualify.

Partnerships which include partial city ownership will qualify as long as the store is substantively under City of New York control. Partnerships with non-profit or other operators which don’t include direct city ownership of the store will not qualify. 

Only stores that are initiated, approved, or opened during Zohran Mamdani’s mayoralty and that are described in credible reporting or official City communications as part of the “city-owned grocery store” initiative associated with his campaign platform will qualify. Grocery stores created by previous or subsequent administrations, or any other government-run or subsidized retail programs that are not reasonably attributable to the Mamdani administration’s city-owned grocery store policy, will not qualify.

If Mamdani is confirmed to have lost the 2025 NYC Mayoral election by a consensus of credible reporting, this market will immediately resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

question: Will Mamdani freeze NYC rents before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if both the following occur:

1. Zohran Mamdani wins the 2025 NYC Mayoral election.

2. The New York City Rent Guidelines Board implements rent adjustments at 0.0 % for both one-year and two-year renewal leases for rent-stabilized apartments citywide by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The policy will be considered implemented only if a 0.0 % increases for both one-year and two-year renewal leases is in effect by the resolution date. An announced intention or proposed order not yet effective will not qualify. Orders that are blocked, enjoined, or otherwise prevented from taking effect by the deadline will not qualify.

If the policy is enacted through another official mechanism, including but not limited to a mayoral executive order, local legislation, or state law, and goes into effect by the resolution date it will qualify.

Orders that apply only to one lease term (e.g., 0 % on one-year but > 0 % on two-year), apply only to specific unit types (e.g., hotels or SROs), or relate to non-stabilized units will not qualify. Policies which include limited exceptions — such as exclusions for specific categories of rent-stabilized units (e.g., hardship exemptions, temporarily exempt buildings, or administrative carveouts) — will still qualify as long as a general policy of 0 % rent adjustment for both one-year and two-year renewal leases on rent-stabilized apartments and lofts is in effect citywide.

If Mamdani is confirmed to have lost the 2025 NYC Mayoral election by a consensus of credible reporting, this market will immediately resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting and official Rent Guidelines Board materials.

eventMetadata.context_description: Assemblymember Zohran Mamdani's persistent advocacy for a New York City rent freeze, including his sponsorship of tenant protection bills and leadership in protests against recent Rent Guidelines Board hikes, drives the 55.5% yes probability on Polymarket. Traders weigh his influence within the Democratic-controlled state legislature and growing progressive pressure amid tenant rallies, such as his July 2024 arrest during a demonstration outside City Hall. While the board approved a 2.75% increase for one-year leases this year, historical precedents like the 2015 freeze and upcoming annual adjustments through 2026 fuel optimism for a zero-increase outcome under Mamdani's push before 2027, balanced against landlord opposition and fiscal constraints.

question: Israel x Hamas ceasefire cancelled by March 31, 2026?
description:
On October 9, Israel and Hamas signed a deal implementing a ceasefire: https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/israel-hamas-agree-gaza-ceasefire-return-hostages-2025-10-09/

This market will resolve to "Yes" if a either Israel or Hamas announce the cancellation of the ongoing ceasefire, or if a consensus of credible reporting confirms that the ceasefire is no longer in effect, by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

Announcements that one side or another violated the ongoing ceasefire will not be enough to resolve this market to "Yes"; only a definitive announcement by Israel or Hamas, or otherwise a wide consensus of credible reporting that that the ceasefire has been ended, will qualify to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary solution resolution source will be official information from Hamas or the government of Israel, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Will Trump issue a Gold Card by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the first U.S. "Gold Card" visa, is officially issued by March 31, 2026, at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

Any new visa or pathway to citizenship which is granted as part of a new program created by the Trump Administration after February 26, 2025, which is either referred to as a Gold Card, or involves a new pathway to U.S. citizenship, work permits, or any other form of legal residency status in exchange for payments or investment will qualify.

The primary resolution source will be official information form the Trump administration however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors "No" at 99.4% implied probability for President-elect Trump issuing a gold card by March 31, driven by the absence of any concrete steps toward implementation since his December 2024 announcement of a $5 million gold card program to replace the EB-5 investor visa. With inauguration on January 20, the roughly 10-week window leaves insufficient time for regulatory changes, congressional buy-in, or operational rollout typically required for new immigration pathways. No official executive orders, agency preparations, or first issuances have materialized, reinforcing trader confidence in delay. A surprise fast-tracked order post-inauguration remains a slim possibility, though historical precedents for such rapid policy launches are rare.

question: Will Trump be impeached by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes“ if the US House of Representatives by simple majority vote to approve or pass one or more articles of impeachment of President Donald Trump, by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Neither trial nor conviction by the US Senate, nor removal from office, is necessary to resolve this market to “Yes“.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from the federal government of the United States, however a consensus of credible reporting will be used.

Traders eye January congressional organization as a key watchpoint.

events.title: Israel and Saudi Arabia normalize relations by March 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Israel and the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia officially announce the establishment of diplomatic relations by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Israel and the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Saudi Arabia's insistence on a credible path to Palestinian statehood as a precondition for normalizing ties with Israel remains the dominant barrier, reinforced by the ongoing Gaza conflict and lack of diplomatic breakthroughs. Traders' near-unanimous 99.3% "No" consensus reflects no official announcements, stalled U.S.-mediated talks, and Riyadh's public rejections amid regional tensions including Houthi attacks and Lebanon clashes. Historical Abraham Accords expansions faced similar hurdles without Palestinian progress, and with the March 31 deadline approaching, momentum is absent. Realistic shifts could stem from an improbable Gaza ceasefire or surprise U.S. pressure during the presidential transition, though evidence points to continued impasse.

question: Israel and Saudi Arabia normalize relations before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Israel and the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia officially announce the establishment of diplomatic relations by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

events.title: Israel and Saudi Arabia normalize relations before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Israel and the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia officially announce the establishment of diplomatic relations by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Israel and the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The ongoing Israel-Hamas war in Gaza remains the primary barrier to normalization between Israel and Saudi Arabia before 2027, as Riyadh continues to condition any deal on concrete progress toward Palestinian statehood and a sustainable ceasefire. Pre-October 2023 talks, advanced under U.S. mediation as part of Abraham Accords expansion, collapsed amid the conflict, with Saudi officials repeatedly reaffirming their stance in recent statements, including Foreign Minister Faisal bin Farhan's November 2024 remarks tying normalization to regional stability. Heightened regional tensions, including Houthi attacks and Saudi Arabia's warming ties with Iran via China-brokered deals, further dim prospects. Traders' 78% "No" consensus reflects this entrenched impasse, despite potential U.S. policy shifts post-election, underscoring the wisdom of crowds in pricing political risks.

question: Ukraine signs peace deal with Russia by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes”  if, Ukraine signs any written instrument (e.g., treaty, ceasefire/armistice, framework/“roadmap,” exchange of letters, or mediated agreement text) that: (i) includes both Ukraine and the Russian Federation as parties, and (ii) either ends hostilities/establishes a ceasefire or commits both sides to a defined process toward ending the war (i.e., stated objective of peace/normalization plus principles, steps, and/or a timetable) by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

Only Ukraine’s signature is required; Russia’s signature or ratification is not.

Localized, temporary, or issue-specific arrangements—such as airstrike-limitation or deconfliction protocols, humanitarian pauses, evacuation corridors, prisoner-exchange or trade/export arrangements, border/DMZ adjustments, or ceasefires limited to a particular sector/front/municipality—will not qualify.

The document must bear a wet-ink or officially issued electronic signature of an authorized Ukrainian representative. Unsigned agreements (e.g., the 2023 Ohrid arrangement) will not qualify regardless of if they are otherwise officially enacted. 

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' near-unanimous 99% consensus against a Ukraine-Russia peace deal by March 31 stems from the complete absence of active bilateral negotiations, with both Kyiv and Moscow issuing firm public rejections of short-term compromises. Recent Russian advances in Donetsk and Ukraine's deep strikes into Russian territory underscore escalating frontline intensity, far from de-escalation signals. Official statements from President Zelenskyy demanding full territorial restoration and President Putin's insistence on recognizing annexed regions highlight irreconcilable red lines. While U.S. President-elect Trump's post-inauguration diplomacy could catalyze indirect talks, logistical hurdles and domestic ratification needs make a binding accord by late March implausible absent a dramatic battlefield reversal or surprise concession.

question: Will Hezbollah disarm by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Hezbollah officially announces it will disarm in Lebanon by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only announcements supported by the Secretary-General of Hezbollah (currently Naim Qassem), a direct successor, or, if the position of Secretary-General of Hezbollah is vacant, the widely acknowledged leadership of Hezbollah will qualify. 

For the purposes of this market, "disarm" refers to a public commitment to relinquish or dismantle its military, whether partially or completely, in Lebanon.

Announcements of partial disarmament (e.g., surrendering a class of weapons or agreeing to disarm in stages or a certain region) will qualify as long as it is part of an acknowledged disarmament process.

Only official announcements will qualify. Informal statements, plans contingent on future conditions, statements of intent without a formal policy directive, or any other statements that do not constitute a formal policy announcement will not be considered.

Primary resolution sources will include official statements from Hezbollah leadership; however, a wide consensus of credible reporting confirming a policy of disarmament has been instituted will also qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probability for Hezbollah disarming by the specified date, driven primarily by the November 27, 2024, U.S.-brokered ceasefire with Israel, which mandates Hezbollah's withdrawal north of the Litani River and Lebanese Army deployment in the south but stops short of requiring full disarmament. Recent Israeli strikes decimated Hezbollah's leadership, including Hassan Nasrallah, and degraded its rocket arsenal, weakening the group militarily yet not politically, as it retains parliamentary seats and Iranian backing. Enforcement hinges on UNIFIL monitoring under Resolution 1701, with potential violations or U.S. diplomatic pressure as key upcoming catalysts amid Lebanon's fragile government.

question: NATO article 5 before 2027?
description:
If any NATO member country invokes Article 5 of the North Atlantic Treaty at any time between November 5, 2025 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from NATO (nato.int), however a consensus of credible media will also suffice.

events.title: NATO article 5 before 2027?
events.description:

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from NATO (nato.int), however a consensus of credible media will also suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on "No" at 77.5% for NATO Article 5 invocation before 2027 reflects the absence of any direct armed attack on alliance territory, a threshold unmet since the 2001 post-9/11 activation. Russia's war in Ukraine, while escalating hybrid threats like Baltic airspace incursions, remains confined outside NATO borders, with Moscow's forces depleted and alliance deterrence bolstered by Finland and Sweden's 2023-2024 accessions, record-high defense spending, and forward deployments on the eastern flank. Recent NATO summits reaffirmed unity amid U.S. leadership transitions, while no credible escalations—such as territorial incursions or major cyber-physical assaults—have materialized, underscoring low near-term collective defense trigger risk despite rhetorical tensions.

question: Yoon out of custody by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President of South Korea Yoon Suk Yeol is released from custody by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Yoon is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Yoon is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Yoon to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Will China unban Bitcoin by 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the government of the People's Republic of China (PRC) explicitly announces that Chinese citizens will be allowed to legally buy Bitcoin with yuan (renminbi) from inside China by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For this market to resolve to "Yes" it is only necessary that the PRC announces this change will take place. Whether it actually does will have no bearing on the resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the PRC, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

question: Will Donald Trump visit the United Kingdom in 2026?
description:
If U.S. President Donald Trump visits a listed country between January 1 and December 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, the relevant market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Trump physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the listed country. Whether or not Trump enters the country's airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from government of the United States of America, official information from Trump or released by his verified social media accounts (e.g. https://twitter.com/POTUS), however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket for Donald Trump's 2026 international visits hinges on his first-term patterns and emerging second-term foreign policy signals, with no official itinerary announced as of early 2025. High implied probabilities favor traditional allies like Saudi Arabia—site of his 2017 debut trip—and Israel amid ongoing Middle East tensions, while Mexico and Canada reflect trade and border priorities under USMCA renewal talks. Recent cabinet picks, including foreign policy hawks like Marco Rubio for State, boost odds for confrontational destinations like China or Ukraine, but geopolitical flux from Ukraine aid debates and NATO summits could shift dynamics. Key upcoming events include the G7 summit (host TBD) and potential bilateral deals post-inauguration.

question: Will Trump sell 0 Gold Cards in 2026?
description:
Donald Trump has announced a “Gold Card” plan to offer a pathway citizenship to citizenship in exchange for a $5m payment.

This market will resolve according to the number of individuals who purchase a “Gold Card” between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

Any individual participating in a new program created after February 26, 2025, by the Trump Administration which is either referred to as a Gold Card, or involves a new pathway to U.S. citizenship, work permits, or any other form of legal residency status in exchange for payments or investment will qualify. 

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source will be official information from the Trump Administration. If the Trump Administration does not publish the number of individuals who receive Gold Cards by December 31, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader skepticism dominates the market on Trump's proposed $5 million Gold Card visas, with zero sales at 22.5% and 1-100 at 19.7% reflecting doubts over rapid implementation post-inauguration, as the program requires executive rulemaking and potential congressional input to supplant EB-5 pathways. Recent podcast remarks by Trump outlined the green card-to-citizenship pitch targeting ultra-wealthy immigrants for revenue, but no DHS transition plans or application frameworks have emerged, fueling uncertainty on demand amid economic headwinds and legal challenges. Tight clustering stems from absent catalysts; separation could arise from January policy directives, first approvals, or high-profile buyer commitments boosting mid-range bins like 1k-2.5k.

This market will resolve according to the number of individuals who purchase a “Gold Card” between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

Any individual participating in a new program created after February 26, 2025, by the Trump Administration which is either referred to as a Gold Card, or involves a new pathway to U.S. citizenship, work permits, or any other form of legal residency status in exchange for payments or investment will qualify. 

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source will be official information from the Trump Administration. If the Trump Administration does not publish the number of individuals who receive Gold Cards by December 31, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader skepticism dominates the market on Trump's proposed $5 million Gold Card visas, with zero sales at 22.5% and 1-100 at 19.7% reflecting doubts over rapid implementation post-inauguration, as the program requires executive rulemaking and potential congressional input to supplant EB-5 pathways. Recent podcast remarks by Trump outlined the green card-to-citizenship pitch targeting ultra-wealthy immigrants for revenue, but no DHS transition plans or application frameworks have emerged, fueling uncertainty on demand amid economic headwinds and legal challenges. Tight clustering stems from absent catalysts; separation could arise from January policy directives, first approvals, or high-profile buyer commitments boosting mid-range bins like 1k-2.5k.
